definition: To discuss something in order to reach an agreement
exampleSentence: We need to negotiate the terms of the contract before signing.
exampleTranslation: 签约前我们需要协商合同条款。

definition: The total income earned by a business or organization
exampleSentence: The company's annual revenue exceeded $10 million last year.
exampleTranslation: 该公司去年年收入超过了1000万美元。

definition: A detailed plan for achieving success in a particular situation
exampleSentence: The marketing team developed a new strategy to increase brand awareness.
exampleTranslation: 营销团队制定了新策略来提升品牌知名度。

definition: A person or group with an interest or concern in a business
exampleSentence: All stakeholders were invited to the quarterly review meeting.
exampleTranslation: 所有利益相关者都被邀请参加季度评审会议。

definition: To work together with one or more people on a project
exampleSentence: Our team collaborated with the design department on the new product.
exampleTranslation: 我们团队与设计部门合作开发新产品。

definition: To put a plan or system into effect
exampleSentence: The company plans to implement the new policy next quarter.
exampleTranslation: 公司计划在下个季度实施新政策。

definition: The latest time or date by which something should be completed
exampleSentence: We need to finish the report before Friday's deadline.
exampleTranslation: 我们需要在周五的截止日期前完成报告。

definition: Achieving maximum productivity with minimum wasted effort
exampleSentence: The new software makes our workflow much more efficient.
exampleTranslation: 新软件使我们的工作流程高效得多。

definition: The buying of one company by another or the gaining of an asset
exampleSentence: The acquisition of the smaller company was completed in March.
exampleTranslation: 对那家小公司的收购在三月份完成。

definition: A standard or point of reference for comparing or measuring things
exampleSentence: Their customer service sets the benchmark for the industry.
exampleTranslation: 他们的客户服务为行业树立了标杆。

definition: The act of obeying rules, regulations, or standards
exampleSentence: All departments must ensure compliance with the new data protection regulations.
exampleTranslation: 所有部门必须确保遵守新的数据保护法规。

definition: To give a task or responsibility to someone else
exampleSentence: A good manager knows how to delegate tasks effectively.
exampleTranslation: 好的管理者知道如何有效地委派任务。

definition: A range of investments or products held by a person or company
exampleSentence: The investment portfolio includes stocks, bonds, and real estate.
exampleTranslation: 投资组合包括股票、债券和房地产。

definition: Able to be changed in size or scale to meet demand
exampleSentence: We need a scalable solution that can grow with the business.
exampleTranslation: 我们需要一个能随业务增长的可扩展解决方案。

definition: To use something to maximum advantage
exampleSentence: We should leverage our existing customer base to promote the new product.
exampleTranslation: 我们应该利用现有客户群来推广新产品。
